reviews.review_msg: has strange assignments and does not explain what she is looking for in them, she was okey over all, but I would try to avoid taking another class with her

Ironically, I did better in this course when I was bored than the last course with her than I got into.  But I also think she was more focused and impartial in her teaching here than when I did her course on free will.  She seemed to have here own axe to grind in that course...not so much with this one.
She is a pretty good professor. The classes can get a little dull at times but they were fine overall. Class was best when people got involved and asked questions.

Try to avoid! This was one class that I was really looking forward to, but I was really disappointed. Her lectures are really boring, and hard to comprehend. She does not give any direction on what she is looking for in assignments and then rips your paper apart (with comments) when she returns it.

Pros: She's very knowledgeable prof, and never failed to keep me interested.
 Cons: Very strange marking system.  She took a blessed amount of time to get us back our assignments, and had a very odd system of marking.  I'll never understand how I can get an A- and hardly receive ANY comments.

She's very kind, however she misses a lot of classes and then the students are the ones to pay, because we have to make up all the lost time. Since she is often away, she assigns things very late, and then we have as little as 5 days in which to complete an assignment. It's not really fair, and it's quite stressful. Not worth it.

Kirstie is very nice and sweet. She encourages students to ask questions if they are confused. She can be a little boring, but it's a lot to do with the material, which I wasn't interested in. I found her to be a generous marker. She gave me an A+ on an essay that I would've given myself an A on.
she built my interest in Philosophy =)
I liked her very much. Clever, fun, and kind.
